---
title: "Untitled"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-01-11"
---


```{r }
setwd('/Users/huangyichen/Desktop/UCHI-QUARTER/2026winter/STATS 2/homework/dataset')
ohie_data <- read.csv("OHIE.CSV")
head(ohie_data) #To check dataset
```

###QUESTION 1
```{r }
options(scipen = 999)
#define baseline
baseline_vars <- c("numhh_list", "female", "age", "race_white", 
                   "hs_degree", "college_degree", "health_baseline")
#create an new frame to set outcome (row name)
balance_results <- data.frame(Variable = character(),
                              Control_Mean = numeric(),
                              Treated_Mean = numeric(),
                              Difference = numeric(),
                              P_Value = numeric(),
                              stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#use for loop
for (col_name in baseline_vars) {
  control_data <- ohie_data[ohie_data$treated == 0, col_name]
  treated_data <- ohie_data[ohie_data$treated == 1, col_name]
  mean_c <- mean(control_data, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_t <- mean(treated_data, na.rm = TRUE)
  diff_val <- mean_t - mean_c
  #t-test
  t_test_res <- t.test(treated_data, control_data, var.equal = TRUE)
  #p-value
  p_value <- t_test_res$p.value
  balance_results[nrow(balance_results) + 1, ] <- list(col_name, mean_c, mean_t, diff_val, p_value)
  print("=== Question 1: Balance Table ===")
  print(balance_results)
}
colnames(ohie_data)
```

###QUESTION 3
```{r}
#define variable name
outcome_vars <- c("count_visit_dr", "count_visit_er", "out_of_pocket_spend", "health_score", "happy")
#create an new frame to set outcome (row name)
outcome_results <- data.frame(Variable = character(),
                              Control_Mean = numeric(),
                              Treated_Mean = numeric(),
                              Difference = numeric(),
                              P_Value = numeric(),
                              stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#calculate the for loop
for (col_name in outcome_vars) {
  control_data <- ohie_data[ohie_data$treated == 0, col_name]
  treated_data <- ohie_data[ohie_data$treated == 1, col_name]
  mean_c <- mean(control_data, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_t <- mean(treated_data, na.rm = TRUE)
  diff_val <- mean_t - mean_c
  t_test_res <- t.test(treated_data, control_data, var.equal = TRUE)
  p_value <- t_test_res$p.value
  outcome_results[nrow(outcome_results) + 1, ] <- list(col_name, mean_c, mean_t, diff_val, p_value)
}
#print the outcome
print("=== Question 3: Outcomes Table ===")
print(outcome_results)
```

###QUESTION 4
```{r}
print("=== Question 4: Regression Results ===")
# use for loop
for (col_name in outcome_vars) {
  formula_str <- paste(col_name, "~ treated")
  model <- lm(as.formula(formula_str), data = ohie_data)
  model_summary <- summary(model)
  beta_1 <- model_summary$coefficients[2, 1]
  p_val_reg <- model_summary$coefficients[2, 4]
  cat("\nDependent Variable (Y):", col_name, "\n")
  cat("  -> Regression Beta1 (Diff):", beta_1, "\n")
  cat("  -> Regression P-value:      ", p_val_reg, "\n")
}
```
